statusLine: EU fuel security is under strain with 18 of 27 countries holding critical reserves in at least one fuel type, despite average stock levels remaining above mandatory minimums.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces mounting pressure as oil prices surge 29.6% since late February to $93.95/barrel following Middle East supply disruptions. While EU average reserves of 112.8 days for petrol, 103.1 days for diesel, and 96.7 days for jet fuel remain above the 90-day mandatory minimum, the distribution across member states reveals significant vulnerabilities.

A concerning 18 of 27 EU countries are experiencing critical stock levels in at least one fuel category. Romania faces the most severe shortages with all three fuel types in critical status, holding just 40.4 days of petrol, 30.2 days of diesel, and 76.4 days of jet fuel. Poland and Luxembourg also show critical levels across all categories. Jet fuel appears particularly vulnerable, with 16 countries below safe levels, including major economies like Germany (72.8 days) and France (67.5 days).

The strategic reserve releases implemented in response to Middle East disruptions appear to have provided temporary relief at the aggregate level, but individual country stockpiles remain dangerously low. Eastern European nations are disproportionately affected, with Romania, Poland, and the Baltic states showing particular strain. Price pressures are evident across the bloc, with diesel now averaging €1.87/L and significant regional variations from Malta's €1.34/L petrol to Denmark's €2.50/L.

While Nordic countries like Estonia and Slovenia maintain robust reserves exceeding 365 days for jet fuel, the overall picture suggests European fuel security is increasingly fragmented. The combination of sustained high oil prices and depleted national reserves creates risks for supply continuity, particularly if geopolitical tensions escalate further.
keyPoints: 18 of 27 EU countries hold critical fuel reserves in at least one category despite EU averages exceeding 90-day minimums
Romania faces the most severe crisis with all fuel types critical: petrol 40.4 days, diesel 30.2 days, jet fuel 76.4 days
Jet fuel shows greatest vulnerability with 16 countries below safe levels, including major economies Germany and France
Oil prices surged 29.6% since February disruptions to $93.95/barrel, driving fuel costs to €1.87/L for diesel
Eastern European countries disproportionately affected, while Nordic nations maintain strong reserve buffers
